briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate change expert spokesperson briefing for March 23, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Urgent Warning: Deepening Instability of Earth Systems
sections.content: The World Meteorological Organization (WMO) has warned that the Earth's climate is more unstable than ever, with its balance already disrupted. Greenhouse gas concentrations have reached their highest levels in 2 million years [1, 25]. The Earth's energy imbalance has reached record levels, causing harm to millions, leading to ocean warming and extreme weather events [12, 22, 113]. Specifically, the WMO confirmed that the most recent 11-year period (2015-2025) was the warmest on record, and Arctic sea ice extent also reached its lowest level on record [26, 93, 113]. Experts have warned that this state of global imbalance is termed a 'climate crisis,' with its impacts potentially lasting over 1,000 years, diagnosing the Earth as currently in an emergency state [25, 105].

sections.title: ⚠️ Real Threats of Climate Change and Impacts on Vulnerable Populations
sections.content: Climate change is already having severe global impacts, with Pacific island nations on the front lines, experiencing significant damage and sending a warning message [8]. An analysis suggests that heatwaves in the Western United States would have been virtually impossible without climate change, accompanied by expert diagnoses that extreme weather events bear the 'fingerprint' of the climate crisis [96, 97, 98], and research findings have also been released indicating that climate change is exacerbating the physical inactivity crisis [99]. In East Africa, a five-year consecutive drought has led to 20 million people facing a food crisis, and the tragedy of climate refugees due to floods has occurred [23, 27], while disappearing large bird species highlight the severity of the biodiversity crisis, confirmed through indigenous knowledge [24]. Domestically, researchers at Korea University published findings that climate change increases the risk of cardiovascular disease for people with disabilities, emphasizing that the climate crisis disproportionately affects them [2, 7], and a warning has also been issued that Irish coastal areas must prepare evacuation plans in anticipation of worsening climate change [106].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic and International Climate Change Response Efforts and Carbon Neutrality Initiatives
sections.content: Various domestic and international efforts for climate change and carbon neutrality are ongoing. Among domestic companies, KT&G and Hanwha were selected as leading companies in climate change response and water resource management by CDP (Carbon Disclosure Project), receiving the 'Carbon Management Sector Honors' for three consecutive years [3, 5, 18]. The Korea Institute of Ocean Science and Technology (KIOST) and Pusan National University have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) for joint research and talent development in climate change, strengthening cooperation in crisis response [4, 6, 9, 13, 19, 21, 28]. At the local government level, Wanju County is promoting support for agricultural disaster prevention in response to climate change [10], and the Gimpo Agricultural Technology Center is conducting education to solve the rice seed shortage problem [11], demonstrating practical efforts to address climate change. Furthermore, various initiatives encouraging citizen participation and education are actively underway, including Ulsan Jung-gu's 'Carbon Neutral Two-Cut Challenge' [29, 34, 54], Seosan City's acceleration of resource circulation and carbon neutrality [30, 45, 49], Gangdong-gu's opening of a resource circulation center and carbon neutrality promotion experience hall [37, 41, 42, 57, 62], and Daedeok-gu's operation of a children's carbon neutrality program [32, 39, 46, 53, 59]. In Ulsan, the nation's first specialized carbon neutrality science museum has broken ground [43], and the Youngone Corporation Group is expanding carbon reduction through eco-friendly industrial complex operations and achieving a 61% solar power supply rate in Bangladesh [80, 81].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Current Challenges
sections.content: Domestically and internationally, the importance of renewable energy transition is being highlighted, and related movements are accelerating. The unstable situation in the Middle East is fueling a renewable energy boom [78], and an analysis suggests that the timeline for achieving the 100 GW renewable energy target is accelerating due to the rising prominence of energy independence in response to geopolitical risks [84]. The Korea Power Exchange (KPX) is taking the lead by opening 'Wise Electricity Life,' a nationwide energy saving platform, in anticipation of the renewable energy era [65, 72, 79], and Gunsan City is establishing a renewable energy cooperation system and promoting solar power and ESS projects [64, 68]. The government is fully embarking on the transition to new and renewable energy, injecting supplementary budgets into renewable energy [88, 91], and innovative technological developments, such as the realization of offshore power plants that generate hydrogen by combining wind and wave power, are also underway [89, 90]. However, despite policies to expand renewable energy, criticisms that public power generation companies have allocated only 6% of their total workforce to the renewable energy sector [86] and casualties from a wind turbine fire in Yeongdeok, Gyeongbuk [94] demonstrate that there are challenges to be addressed in the renewable energy transition process, including safety and workforce expansion. Additionally, issues such as the survival of new and renewable energy amidst high oil prices [50, 63, 92], controversies over legalizing renewable energy profit sharing [66], and questions regarding Korea's industrial carbon neutrality strategy [58] are also being raised.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlights the severity of the climate crisis, alongside the coexistence of various domestic and international efforts to overcome it. This is a time that calls for continuous attention and action. This concludes today's climate briefing.
